channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: JD Vance gets his long-awaited moment to admonish Ukraine's Zelenskyy
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Vice President JD Vance was dismissing Ukraine long before he upended an Oval Office meeting Friday by calling Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy “disrespectful” and asking if he had ever thanked the U.S. for its support. When Vance was a candidate for U.S.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration approves major nearly $3 billion arms sale to Israel
item.description: The Trump administration has approved a major nearly $3 billion arms sale to Israel, bypassing a normal congressional review to provide the country with more of the 2,000-pound bombs that it has used in the war against Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Federal workers will get a new email demanding their accomplishments, with a key change
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Federal employees should expect another email on Saturday requiring them to explain their recent accomplishments, a renewed attempt by President Donald Trump and billionaire entrepreneur Elon Musk to demand answers from the government workforce.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Politics Friday: Newly elected lawmakers reflect on their first legislative session
item.description: MPR News host Brian Bakst talks with two state representatives serving in their first legislative session. Later, a conversation with State Auditor Julie Blaha.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Texas A&M System bans drag shows from its universities
item.description: The move potentially sets up a new First Amendment fight between students and university administrators.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jessica Priest, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says Zelenskyy can 'come back when he is ready for Peace' after fiery White House exchange
item.description: An effort to advance a peace deal between Ukraine and Russia came to a halt Friday after President Donald Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy sparred in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he plans to add White House ballroom
item.description: President Trump in a new interview said he is planning to pave part of the Rose Garden, similar to a patio at his Mar-a-Lago property in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Virginia gov promises 'full cooperation' with ICE to deport illegal immigrants
item.description: Virginia became the latest of a handful of mostly GOP states that are compelling law enforcement officers to work with federal authorities to enforce immigration laws.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexican drug lord Rafael Caro Quintero pleads not guilty in 1985 killing of US federal agent
item.description: A Mexican drug lord who was one of U.S. authorities’ most wanted men has been brought into a New York courtroom to answer charges that include orchestrating the 1985 killing of a U.S. federal agent.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Latest: Trump calls for ‘ceasefire now’ between Russia and Ukraine
item.description: President Donald Trump says he wants an “immediate” ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine and has warned Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to make peace or lose American support.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hundreds of weather forecasters fired in latest wave of DOGE cuts
item.description: Hundreds of weather forecasters and other federal National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration employees on probationary status have been fired, lawmakers and weather experts say.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: AG Pam Bondi demands 'full' Epstein files from FBI, vows investigation
item.description: The DOJ released hundreds of Epstein-related files Thursday to a small group of conservative influencers, but Bondi says there's still more to be uncovered.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: OPM outlines stricter accountability for executive employees
item.description: The acting OPM director issued the memo to agency heads after President Donald Trump signed an executive order.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: 2026 gubernatorial race heats up as DeSantis shifts focus to Casey, Trump backs Donalds
item.description: It may appear to be distant, but the 2026 gubernatorial race is already heating up. President Donald Trump has put his full support behind Congressman Byron Donalds, but Gov. Ron DeSantis does not seem to be on board with that choice.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Musk says failure to respond to email a second time will end in termination
item.description: The message appears to contradict recent guidance from the Office of Personnel Management (OPM), which told agency leaders Monday afternoon that employee response to the initial email was not mandatory and that failure to do so would not be considered a resignation.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Trump defends Musk’s ‘genius’ email to federal workers
item.description: President Trump on Monday defended tech billionaire Elon Musk after pushback over his email demanding federal workers list five accomplishments from the past week and in doing so, raising the of risk losing their jobs. “There was a lot of genius in sending it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: AI video of Trump kissing Musk’s feet plays on TVs in HUD building
item.description: A video produced using artificial intelligence (AI) showing President Trump rubbing and kissing Elon Musk's feet played on television screens in the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) building Monday in an apparent mocking of the relationship between the two men.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5
